Title: User Registration with Email and Password
Preconditions: User has valid email address and browser access
Steps: 1. Navigate to registration page | 2. Enter valid email address | 3. Enter strong password (min 8 chars) | 4. Enter full name | 5. Click Register button
Test Data: Email: test@example.com | Password: Test@1234 | Full Name: John Doe
Expected Result: User account is created successfully | User is redirected to menu page | JWT token is stored in localStorage | User receives welcome notification

Title: User Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has registered account
Steps: 1. Navigate to login page | 2. Enter registered email | 3. Enter correct password | 4. Click Login button
Expected Result: User is authenticated successfully | JWT token is generated and stored | User is redirected to menu page | Navigation shows user email and logout button

Title: Browse Menu Items with Search and Filters
Preconditions: Menu items exist in database
Steps: 1. Navigate to menu page | 2. Observe all menu items displayed | 3. Enter search term in search box | 4. Select category from filter dropdown | 5. Verify filtered results
Expected Result: Menu items are displayed in grid layout | Search filters items by name | Category filter shows only items in selected category | Each item shows name price image and availability

Title: Add Item to Cart and Proceed to Checkout
Preconditions: User is logged in and menu items are available
Steps: 1. Browse menu page | 2. Click Add to Cart on desired item | 3. Navigate to Cart page | 4. Verify item is in cart | 5. Update quantity using +/- buttons | 6. Click Proceed to Checkout
Expected Result: Item is added to cart successfully | Cart shows correct item name price and quantity | Total amount is calculated correctly | User can update quantity | Checkout button is enabled

Title: Complete Order Placement with Delivery
Preconditions: User has items in cart and is logged in
Steps: 1. Navigate to checkout page | 2. Select venue from dropdown | 3. Choose Delivery as order mode | 4. Enter delivery address details | 5. Enter city and postal code | 6. Enter phone number | 7. Click Place Order
Test Data: Venue: "Main Restaurant" | Address: "123 Main St" | City: "New York" | Postal Code: "10001" | Phone: "555-1234"
Expected Result: Order is created successfully | Payment is processed (mock) | Order status is set to Received | User is redirected to orders page | Order confirmation notification is sent | Invoice is generated

Title: Complete Food Ordering Flow from Registration to Order Completion
Preconditions: No existing user account
Steps: 1. Register new account with email/password | 2. Browse menu and search for items | 3. Add multiple items to cart with different quantities | 4. Update cart quantities | 5. Remove one item from cart | 6. Proceed to checkout | 7. Fill delivery details | 8. Place order | 9. View order in orders page | 10. Download invoice
Test Data: Email: endtoend@test.com | Password: E2E@Test123 | Items: Pizza x2 Burger x1 Salad x1 | Delivery: 456 Oak St NY 10002
Expected Result: Complete workflow executes successfully | User account is created | Menu browsing works | Cart operations succeed | Order is placed | Payment completes | Invoice PDF is downloadable | Order appears in order history

Title: Admin Menu Management and Order Processing Workflow
Preconditions: Admin account exists with restaurant_admin role
Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Menu Management | 3. Add new menu item with all details | 4. Edit existing menu item | 5. Update item availability | 6. Navigate to Admin Dashboard | 7. View dashboard statistics | 8. Process incoming order by updating status | 9. Mark order as completed
Test Data: Admin Email: admin@restaurant.com | New Item: "Chef Special" Category: "Specials" Price: 15.99 | Order Status Updates: Received -> Preparing -> Ready -> Completed
Expected Result: Admin can access restricted pages | Menu item CRUD operations work | Dashboard shows correct statistics | Order status updates propagate | Notifications are sent on status changes | Real-time stats update

Title: User Attempts Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User account may or may not exist
Steps: 1. Navigate to login page | 2. Enter email address | 3. Enter incorrect password | 4. Click Login button
Expected Result: Error message is displayed | User remains on login page | No JWT token is generated | Error states Invalid email or password

Title: Add Item to Cart When Not Logged In
Preconditions: User is not authenticated
Steps: 1. Navigate to menu page without logging in | 2. Click Add to Cart button on any item
Expected Result: Alert message prompts user to login | Item is not added to cart | User remains on menu page | No cart data is created

Title: Attempt Checkout with Empty Cart
Preconditions: User is logged in but cart is empty
Steps: 1. Login to application | 2. Navigate directly to checkout page without adding items
Expected Result: Appropriate error message is shown | Order creation fails gracefully | User is informed cart is empty | Redirect to menu page or show empty cart message

Title: Search Menu with No Matching Results
Preconditions: Menu items exist in database
Steps: 1. Navigate to menu page | 2. Enter search term that matches no items | 3. Verify results
Expected Result: No items are displayed | Appropriate empty state message is shown | Search input remains functional | Category filter still accessible

Title: Update Cart Item Quantity to Zero
Preconditions: User has items in cart
Steps: 1. Navigate to cart page | 2. Click minus button to reduce quantity to zero | 3. Verify item removal
Test Data: Cart Item: Any item with quantity 1
Expected Result: Item is removed from cart when quantity reaches zero | Cart total updates correctly | If last item UI shows empty cart state

Title: Reorder from Order History
Preconditions: User has completed previous orders
Steps: 1. Login to application | 2. Navigate to Orders page | 3. Find completed order | 4. Click Reorder button | 5. Navigate to cart
Test Data: Previous Order ID: Any completed order
Expected Result: All items from previous order are added to cart | Cart shows correct items and quantities | User can modify cart before checkout | Previous order remains in history unchanged

Title: Download Invoice for Completed Order
Preconditions: User has order with completed payment status
Steps: 1. Login to application | 2. Navigate to Orders page | 3. Find order with completed payment | 4. Click Download Invoice button
Test Data: Order ID: Any order with payment_status = completed
Expected Result: PDF invoice is generated | File downloads successfully | Invoice contains order details items totals | Invoice number and date are present

Title: Admin Updates Order Status Through Dashboard
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in and orders exist
Steps: 1. Login as admin/staff | 2. Navigate to Admin Dashboard | 3. Locate active order | 4. Select new status from dropdown | 5. Verify update
Expected Result: Order status updates in database | Customer receives notification of status change | Dashboard reflects new status immediately | Order appears in correct status filter

Title: Filter Menu Items by Category and Dietary Preference
Preconditions: Menu items have categories and dietary tags
Steps: 1. Navigate to menu page | 2. Select category filter | 3. Observe filtered results | 4. Test multiple categories | 5. Combine with search
Expected Result: Only items matching selected category are displayed | Filter combinations work correctly | Clear filter returns all items | UI updates responsively

Title: Register with Already Existing Email
Preconditions: User account already exists with given email
Steps: 1. Navigate to registration page | 2. Enter email of existing user | 3. Enter password and other details | 4. Click Register
Expected Result: Registration fails with appropriate error | Error message states Email already registered | User is not created | Existing account remains unchanged

Title: Access Admin Dashboard as Regular Customer
Preconditions: User is logged in with customer role
Steps: 1. Login as customer user | 2. Attempt to navigate to /admin URL | 3. Attempt to navigate to /menu-management URL
Expected Result: Access is denied | Error message Access denied Admin/Staff only is shown | User cannot view dashboard statistics | User cannot modify menu items or orders

Title: Submit Order Without Required Delivery Details
Preconditions: User has items in cart and selects delivery mode
Steps: 1. Navigate to checkout page | 2. Select Delivery as order mode | 3. Leave address fields empty | 4. Click Place Order
Test Data: Order Mode: Delivery | Address: empty | City: empty | Postal Code: empty
Expected Result: Form validation prevents submission | Required field indicators are shown | Error messages displayed for missing fields | Order is not created

Title: Attempt to Modify Menu Item as Customer
Preconditions: Customer user is logged in
Steps: 1. Login as customer | 2. Attempt to access /menu-management | 3. Try API calls to menu modification endpoints
Expected Result: Customer cannot access menu management page | API returns 403 Forbidden | RBAC middleware blocks unauthorized access | Menu items remain unchanged

Title: Place Order for Unavailable Menu Items
Preconditions: Menu item is_available flag is set to false
Steps: 1. Menu item is marked unavailable in database | 2. User attempts to add unavailable item to cart | 3. Verify prevention mechanism
Expected Result: Add to Cart button shows Unavailable label | Item cannot be added to cart | User sees visual indication item is unavailable | Order cannot include unavailable items

Title: Responsive UI on Mobile Device
Preconditions: Application is accessible on mobile browser
Steps: 1. Open application on mobile device or browser dev tools | 2. Navigate through all pages | 3. Test menu browsing | 4. Test cart operations | 5. Test forms | 6. Verify touch interactions
Expected Result: All pages render correctly on mobile | Navigation is touch-friendly | Forms are usable on small screens | Grid layouts adapt responsively | Text is readable without zooming | Buttons are adequately sized

Title: Responsive UI on Tablet Device
Preconditions: Application is accessible on tablet browser
Steps: 1. Open application on tablet or set browser to tablet viewport | 2. Navigate all pages | 3. Test grid layouts | 4. Verify menu and cart UI | 5. Test admin dashboard on tablet
Expected Result: UI adapts to medium screen sizes | Multi-column layouts display properly | Touch targets are appropriate size | Dashboard statistics grid formats correctly | Modal dialogs are centered and sized appropriately

Title: Multi-Venue Order Placement
Preconditions: Multiple venues exist in system
Steps: 1. Login to application | 2. Browse menu for Venue A | 3. Add items to cart | 4. Proceed to checkout | 5. Select Venue A | 6. Complete order | 7. Repeat for Venue B
Test Data: Venue A: Main Restaurant | Venue B: Cloud Kitchen | Items: Different menu items per venue
Expected Result: Each venue has distinct menu items | Cart is venue-specific | Orders are associated with correct venue | Admin can filter orders by venue | Venue selection works in checkout

Title: Notification System for Order Status Updates
Preconditions: User has placed order
Steps: 1. Place new order | 2. Admin updates order status | 3. Navigate to notifications (if UI exists) | 4. Verify notifications are created
Test Data: Order Status Changes: Received -> Preparing -> Ready -> Out for Delivery
Expected Result: Notification is created on order placement | Status update generates notification | Notifications visible to user | Notification types include order_confirmation order_status_update payment_success
